speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone can be everything now.
zh: 每个人现在都可以成为任何角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: PMs can ship code, designers can write PRDs, engineers can product, and there's this confusion and frustration of what is my job anymore.
zh: 产品经理（PM）可以交付代码，设计师可以写产品需求文档（PRD），工程师可以做产品，还有这种困惑和挫败感，即我的工作到底是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anytime a new technology comes along, you go through a storming phase before you go through the forming phase of things.
zh: 每当一项新技术出现时，在进入定型阶段之前，你会经历一个动荡阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are in the middle of that right now.
zh: 我们现在正处于这个阶段之中。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think that means we should put AI back into the box and say, "Let's not use it."
zh: 我不这意味着我们应该把 AI 重新放回盒子里说，“我们不要使用它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: If we all become builders, will we still need separate functions?
zh: 如果我们都成为构建者，我们还需要独立的职能部门吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I still see a craft excellence that's really important that I don't think is going away anytime soon.
zh: 我仍然看到一种非常重要的卓越工艺，我认为它短期内不会消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: I still find great engineering to be scarce, great data science to be scarce, great creativity to be scarce.
zh: 我仍然觉得优秀的工程是稀缺的，优秀的数据科学是稀缺的，卓越的创造力是稀缺的。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you look at the early culture deck of Netflix, high agency, autonomy, paying top of market, this is what I hear constantly now from how the top AI labs operate.
zh: 如果你看看 Netflix 的早期文化手册，高能动性、自主权、支付市场顶薪，这就是我现在从顶级 AI 实验室的运作方式中不断听到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Netflix's culture has always been excellence as an operating system.
zh: Netflix 的文化一直是以卓越作为操作系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a resistance to do the thing that a lot of bigger companies would do and to feel comfortable in that discomfort very often.
zh: 它是对抗许多大公司会做的事情，并且经常在那种不适中感到舒适。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are the ingredients to make this happen?
zh: 实现这一点的要素是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Talent density is the non-negotiable, being very comfortable with risk-taking in cases where things are not going well and not assume that process is going to fix it.
zh: 人才密度是不可商量的，在事情不顺利的情况下对承担风险感到非常自在，并且不要假设流程能解决它。

speaker: Unknown
en: What have you added to the career ladders within this AI world?
zh: 在这个 AI 世界里，你们在职业阶梯中增加了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: We need more systems thinkers, people who can look across all the business domains and abstract that to here's the building blocks we're going to need.
zh: 我们需要更多的系统思考者，那些能够跨越所有业务领域审视并将其抽象为我们需要的构建模块的人。

speaker: Lenny
en: How do people learn this?
zh: 人们如何学习这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: Small trick.
zh: 一个小技巧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Each problem you're trying to solve, step out one click to the what am I assuming is true about the broader space.
zh: 对于你试图解决的每个问题，向外退一步思考，我对更广阔的空间假设什么是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Elizabeth Stone, Product and Technology Officer at Netflix.
zh: 今天，我的嘉宾是 Elizabeth Stone，Netflix 的产品与技术官。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is Elizabeth's second visit to the podcast.
zh: 这是 Elizabeth 第二次做客播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Her first visit when she was just the CTO was for the longest time, one of the most popular episodes of this podcast.
zh: 她第一次做客时她只是 CTO，在很长一段时间里，是这期播客最受欢迎的剧集之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: You'll soon see why.
zh: 你很快就会明白为什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is such a killer conversation, because when we chatted two and a half years ago, AI was only starting to emerge.
zh: 这是一次非常棒的对话，因为我们在两年半前聊天时，AI 才刚刚开始出现。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as a long time head of engineering and product and data science, Elizabeth has such a unique perspective on where things are heading and what's worth paying attention to.
zh: 作为工程、产品和数据科学的长期负责人，Elizabeth 对事物的发展方向以及什么值得关注的看法非常独特。

speaker: Unknown
en: Prior to Netflix, Elizabeth was VP of Science at Lyft, Chief Operating Officer at Nuna, an economist at the Analysis Group and a trader at Merrill Lynch.
zh: 在加入 Netflix 之前，Elizabeth 是 Lyft 的科学副总裁，Nuna 的首席运营官，Analysis Group 的经济学家，以及美林证券的交易员。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before we get into it, don't forget to check out lennysproductpass.com for an entire year free of the hottest and best crafted AI products in the world, available exclusively to Lenny's Newsletter subscribers.
zh: 在开始之前，别忘了访问 lennysproductpass.com 免费获取一整年全球最热门、最精心制作的 AI 产品，专供 Lenny 的时事通讯订阅者使用。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I bring you Elizabeth Stone.
zh: 话不多说，我请来 Elizabeth Stone。

speaker: Unknown
en: Elizabeth, thank you so much for being here and welcome back to the podcast.
zh: Elizabeth，非常感谢你来到这里，欢迎回到播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you.
zh: 谢谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm honored to be here, once and now twice.
zh: 我很荣幸能来这里，一次，现在是两次。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a rare treat for me.
zh: 这对我来说是一次难得的享受。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if you know this, but your first visit to the podcast, your episode ended up being my second most popular episode.
zh: 你可能不知道，你第一次做客播客时，你的那一集曾是我受欢迎度排名第二的剧集。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're right behind Brian Chesky for the longest time.
zh: 在很长一段时间里，你紧排在 Brian Chesky 之后。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow.
zh: 哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am pleasantly surprised and also mildly competitive of how do I get to the first spot.
zh: 我感到惊喜，也有点好胜心，想着怎么才能拿到第一名。

speaker: Lenny
en: [inaudible 00:02:58], I guess.

speaker: Unknown
en: But I'll set that aside for now.
zh: 但我现在先把那个放一边。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is our shot.
zh: 这是我们的机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Brian's amazing, so I'll let that one go.
zh: Brian 很棒，所以我放过那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, he is.
zh: 是的，他很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there's just all these fancy AI people that are just coming in hot.
zh: 然后还有所有这些花哨的 AI 人员正在涌入。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it's been two and a half years at this point.
zh: 所以，到现在已经两年半了。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot's changed.
zh: 改变很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously, AI.
zh: 显然，AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something AI is allowing people to do is everyone can be everything now.
zh: AI 让人们能做的事情之一是，每个人现在都可以成为任何角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: This idea of PMs can ship code, designers can write PRDs, and engineers can product and everyone's everything.
zh: 这种想法是产品经理可以交付代码，设计师可以写产品需求文档，工程师可以做产品，而且每个人都是全能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a bunch of elements of this conversation.
zh: 这段对话有很多方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is that I've heard from people that there's also this kind of confusion and frustration of what is my job anymore.
zh: 一个是我听人们说，也有这种困惑和挫败感，即我的工作到底是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: What am I responsible for as a PM, as a designer?
zh: 作为产品经理，作为设计师，我负责什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that something you've experienced?
zh: 那是你经历过的事情吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I hear it within Netflix for sure.
zh: 我在 Netflix 内部肯定听到过这种说法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think anytime a new technology comes along, especially one that's as transformative as GenAI, you go through a storming phase before you go through the forming phase of things.
zh: 我认为每当一项新技术出现时，尤其是像 GenAI 这样具有变革性的技术，在进入「形成」阶段之前，你会经历一个「动荡」阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we are in the middle of that right now.
zh: 我认为我们正处于这个阶段的中间。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think that means we should put AI back into the box and say, let's not use it, because this is complicating all of our preconceived notions about our roles.
zh: 我不认为这意味着我们应该把 AI 塞回盒子里并说，让我们不要使用它，因为它使我们对角色的所有先入为主的观念变得复杂化了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I do think it means we have to be much more thoughtful about how do we get the benefits while reducing the costs.
zh: 但我确实认为这意味着我们必须更加深思熟虑地思考，如何才能在降低成本的同时获得收益。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's a great thing that people are experimenting with.
zh: 我认为人们正在尝试是一件好事。

speaker: Unknown
en: How can I develop an idea faster, prototype an idea, put together an initial set of code that would allow us to test it?
zh: 我怎样才能更快地构思想法，制作原型，并整理出一组能够让我们测试它的初始代码？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do I believe that means anyone should be shipping code to production, that everyone should actually be doing everything?
zh: 我相信这意味着任何人都应该将代码发布到生产环境吗，每个人实际上都应该做所有事情吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Probably not, but I think that it's good for people to be exploring what's possible.
zh: 可能不，但我认为人们去探索什么是可能的，这是好事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then like I mentioned earlier, the benefit of having product and tech teams together, is that if the business problem is clear, I think it's okay and it's healthy for there to be some fluidity in the roles that people play.
zh: 然后就像我之前提到的，拥有产品和科技团队在一起的好处是，如果业务问题清晰，我认为人们所扮演的角色有一些流动性是可以的，也是健康的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because instead of having to wait for the engineering team to be ready, to be able to prototype something, product and design can move faster on it, but they should still work with their engineering partner to think through, how should we productize this?
zh: 因为与其等待工程团队准备好、能够去原型化某些东西，产品和设计可以更快地行动，但他们仍然应该与他们的工程合作伙伴合作思考，我们应该如何将这个产品化？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we scale it?
zh: 我们如何扩展它？

speaker: Unknown
en: What are the guardrails for it?
zh: 它的护栏是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I don't think it makes the functional expertise obsolete.
zh: 所以，我不认为这会让专业技能过时。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it means that teams have to be more comfortable with maybe this helps us move faster in a certain direction.
zh: 我认为这意味着团队必须更舒服于也许这能帮我们在某个方向上移动得更快。

speaker: Unknown
en: From an organizational perspective, things I think about to make this more coherent or less frustrating, are some of the things that have to be in place for us to get the benefits rather than the cost.
zh: 从组织的角度来看，为了让这更连贯或更少挫败感，我认为需要落实一些事情，以便我们获得收益而不是成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that includes clarity on source of truth data, guardrails on shipping code to production or testing, before we make large changes.
zh: 所以，这包括对真相来源数据的明确，在发布代码到生产环境或测试之前的护栏，在我们做出重大变更之前。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thinking about opportunities where we can trust the output of AI versus we should have a process or review that helps us check that we're getting high quality outcomes.
zh: 思考哪些机会我们可以信任 AI 的输出，对比哪些地方我们应该有一个流程或审查来帮助我们检查我们是否获得了高质量的成果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the importance of reiterating that humans are still responsible for what happens.
zh: 以及重申人类仍然对发生的事情负责的重要性。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it can be that an agent wrote the code or I helped to do an analysis when that's not really my background, but it doesn't make people not have the responsibility that comes with what they've created.
zh: 所以，可能是一个智能体编写了代码，或者我帮助进行了分析，而这并不是我的背景，但这并不意味着人们不需要对他们所创造的东西带来的责任负责。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I think investing in some of those core infrastructure and practices, and reiterating the accountability and responsibility for the outcomes, helps to balance some of what's possible with what we should actually be doing.
zh: 所以，我认为投资于其中一些核心基础设施和实践，并重申对结果的问责和责任，有助于平衡可能性与我们实际应该做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by our season's presenting sponsor, WorkOS.
zh: 本节目由本季度的呈现赞助商 WorkOS 赞助。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do OpenAI, Anthropic, Cursor, Vercel, Replit, Sierra, Clay, and hundreds of other winning companies all have in common?
zh: OpenAI、Anthropic、Cursor、Vercel、Replit、Sierra、Clay 和数百家其他成功的公司有什么共同点？

speaker: Unknown
en: They are all powered by WorkOS.
zh: 它们都由 WorkOS 提供支持。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're building a product for the enterprise, you felt the pain of integrating single sign-on, SCIM, RBAC, audit logs, and other features required by large companies.
zh: 如果你正在为企业构建产品，你一定感受过集成单点登录、SCIM、RBAC、审计日志以及大公司所需的其他功能的痛苦。

speaker: Unknown
en: WorkOS turns those deal blockers into drop-in APIs with a modern developer platform built specifically for B2B SaaS.
zh: WorkOS 利用专为 B2B SaaS 构建的现代开发者平台，将这些交易阻碍变成了现成的 API。

speaker: Unknown
en: Literally every startup that I'm an investor in, that starts to expand upmarket, ends up working with WorkOS, and that's because they are the best.
zh: 几乎每一家的我作为投资者的初创公司，一旦开始向高端市场扩张，最终都会与 WorkOS 合作，因为它们是最好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether you are a seed stage startup trying to land your first enterprise customer or unicorn expanding globally, WorkOS is the fastest path to becoming enterprise ready and unblocking growth.
zh: 无论你是试图获得第一个企业客户的种子阶段初创公司，还是全球扩张的独角兽企业，WorkOS 都是成为企业就绪的最快路径，也是解锁增长的途径。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's essentially Stripe for enterprise features.
zh: 它本质上就是企业功能的 Stripe。

speaker: Unknown
en: Visit workos.com to get started or just hit up their Slack, where they have actual engineers waiting to answer your questions.
zh: 访问 workos.com 开始使用，或者直接联系他们的 Slack，那里有真正的工程师在等待回答你的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: WorkOS allows you to build faster with delightful APIs, comprehensive docs, and a smooth developer experience.
zh: WorkOS 让你能够通过令人愉悦的 API、全面的文档和流畅的开发者体验更快地构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: Go to workos.com to make your app enterprise ready today.
zh: 今天就去 workos.com 让你的应用为企业就绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's really awesome about having you back on the podcast is we chatted before AI was a massive transformation in the world.
zh: 你能再次回到播客上真的很棒，因为我们之前聊天时，AI 还没有在世界上发生巨大的变革。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it's a really cool arc that we can explore here, the shift that we've all gone through.
zh: 所以，这是一个我们可以探索的非常酷的弧线，我们所有人经历过的转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: Coming back to the roles of the product and eng teams, I'm curious how much these roles have changed in the last two and a half years.
zh: 回到产品和工程团队的角色，我很好奇这些角色在过去两年半中发生了多大的变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you think about product engineering, design, data science, user research, which roles have changed most?
zh: 如果你想想产品工程、设计、数据科学、用户研究，哪些角色变化最大？

speaker: Unknown
en: Which roles have changed least?
zh: 哪些角色变化最小？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's most different since two and a half years ago?
zh: 自从两年半前以来最大的不同是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you've mentioned some of the things, so I'll reiterate them and then maybe build.
zh: 所以，你提到了其中一些事情，所以我将重申它们，然后也许再深入。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I have found that PMs, designers, data scientists, are able to get farther in the product development lifecycle before engineering really needs to be front of the line in unlocking things than was true a couple years ago.
zh: 所以，我发现 PM、设计师、数据科学家能够在产品开发生命周期中走得更远，而在几年前，在工程真正需要站在最前面解锁事物之前，情况并非如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: I say that with some caution, because like we were talking about, I don't think it's great to all of a sudden have thousands of prototypes, if they're not aimed at this is an important problem to solve for the business.
zh: 我说这话有些谨慎，因为就像我们之前谈论的那样，我认为如果这些原型不是为了解决对业务重要的问题，那么突然拥有数千个原型并不是一件好事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the engineering partners are aware that we're solving that problem.
zh: 而且工程合作伙伴知道我们正在解决那个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that designers and product managers are going to take the lead in starting to shape the idea, but it's not working in a vacuum and it's not throwing a bunch of spaghetti at the wall to see what sticks.
zh: 设计师和产品经理将带头开始塑造这个想法，但这并不是在真空中工作，也不是把一堆意大利面扔到墙上看看什么能粘住。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when it's the right problem, approach in a thoughtful way with some alignment on that.
zh: 但是当它是正确的问题，以深思熟虑的方式处理并且在达成某种一致的情况下。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've seen product design data science move faster in the direction of let's get to something that's testable on this hypothesis.
zh: 我看到产品设计数据科学在「让我们达到可在这个假设上进行测试」这个方向上移动得更快。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's prototyping, that's writing code.
zh: 所以，这就是原型制作，这就是编写代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other thing I've seen, has been very valuable is we have a lot of information running around in the virtual walls of Netflix.
zh: 我看到的另一件事，非常有价值，是我们有很多信息在 Netflix 的虚拟墙内四处流转。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have experiments we've run over decades.
zh: 我们有几十年来进行的实验。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have insights from consumers.
zh: 我们有来自消费者的洞察。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have input from stakeholders across the business.
zh: 我们有来自整个业务利益相关者的输入。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that was a problem that really presented a challenge of, how do we get the most out of that long history of knowledge and learnings, to say, let's apply that to the problem we've got now to move faster in this is a promising path or this is something that we've learned something about and we could leverage here?
zh: 这确实提出了一个挑战，我们如何充分利用那段悠久的历史知识和经验，比如说，让我们把它应用到现在的问题上以便更快地前进，这是一个有希望的路径，或者这是我们学到一些东西并且我们可以在这里利用的东西？

speaker: Unknown
en: And AI is very powerful at distilling information, looking across a broad set of things, doing an analysis around it, getting to the core of here's some insights to start with.
zh: AI 在提炼信息、广泛查看事物、对其进行分析、直击核心以提供一些初步见解方面非常强大。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would hesitate to rely on that exclusively, but I think it's a head start.
zh: 我会犹豫是否完全依赖它，但我认为这是一个先发优势。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I find even in my own work day-to-day, instead of sending an email that disrupts someone of like, remind me what research did we do in what year and what was the question and what was the test we ran?
zh: 而且我发现即使在我日常的工作中，不再是发送一封打扰某人的电子邮件，比如提醒我我们在哪一年做了什么研究，问题是什么，我们运行的测试是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I can find that almost instantly.
zh: 我几乎可以立即找到那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then I can form my own, here's what I find interesting about this.
zh: 然后我可以形成我自己的观点，这就是我对这个感兴趣的方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've now skipped a couple steps towards, is there something actionable here?
zh: 我现在已经跳过了几个步骤，来到这里是否有可执行的东西？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's data analysis, it's modeling, it's distillation of information.
zh: 所以，那是数据分析，是建模，是信息的提炼。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm seeing more people do that, to your original question.
zh: 关于你最初的问题，我看到越来越多的人这样做了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, instead of that needing to be only the experts who were here for 20 years and saw every experiment or know where to find it, we're now able to do that faster within product and tech across all functions.
zh: 因此，不再需要那些在这里待了 20 年、看过每个实验或知道在哪里找到它的专家，我们现在能够在产品和技术的所有职能部门中更快地做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a big unlock for us is our business stakeholders, sitting in finance and content and advertising, can do that as well.
zh: 对我们的一个重大解锁是我们的业务利益相关者，他们坐在财务、内容和广告部门，也能做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then bring back an initial hypothesis where they want to work more deeply with the data scientist and engineer and so on.
zh: 然后带回一个初步的假设，在这个假设中他们想与数据科学家和工程师等更深入地合作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's something there about the hypothesis generation, prototyping, thinking deeply about problems, that feels like it's accelerating and that functions are able to do that in a more fluid way.
zh: 所以，这里有一些关于假设生成、原型设计、深入思考问题的东西，感觉它在加速，而且职能部门能够以更流畅的方式做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I still see comparative strengths.
zh: 但我仍然看到相对优势。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, data scientists are still going to be experts at, can we trust this data?
zh: 因此，数据科学家仍然是这些方面的专家，我们可以信任这些数据吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are we interpreting it the right way?
zh: 我们是否以正确的方式解释它？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the data versus judgment that we should be applying here?
zh: 我们在这里应该应用的是什么数据，而不是什么判断？

speaker: Unknown
en: A product manager is still going to be exceptional at saying, have we really framed the what of this, the problem we're solving in the right way?
zh: 产品经理在以下方面仍然非常出色，我们是否真正构建了「什么」，即我们以正确方式解决的问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: An engineer still has a craft around the how.
zh: 工程师仍然围绕「如何」有一门手艺。

speaker: Unknown
en: How does this scale?
zh: 这如何扩展？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does high quality look like?
zh: 高质量是什么样的？

speaker: Unknown
en: What problems is this going to create for us based on how we build and deploy something?
zh: 基于我们构建和部署的方式，这会为我们制造什么问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I still see the nuggets of that comparative advantage.
zh: 所以，我仍然看到那种比较优势的精华。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just that we're able to move more fluidly in a lot of steps that normally we would have blockers on.
zh: 只是我们在通常会有阻碍的许多步骤中能够更流畅地行动。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much interesting stuff here.
zh: 这里有很多有趣的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is this last point you made, something I've been thinking about.
zh: 一个是你刚才说的最后一点，这也是我一直在思考的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: If we all become builders, will we still need separate functions?
zh: 如果我们都成为构建者，我们还需要独立的职能部门吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this member of technical staff trend that is happening across AI, where it's like, all right, we don't have a title.
zh: 现在有一种横跨 AI 的技术人员趋势，就像，好吧，我们没有一个头衔。

speaker: Unknown
en: You could be anything.
zh: 你可以是任何东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have to be in a bucket.
zh: 你不必被归入一个桶里。

speaker: Unknown
en: What you're saying here is you believe we will continue to have specialties, product person, engineer, data science, designer.
zh: 你在这里说的是，你相信我们将继续拥有 specialties，产品人员、工程师、数据科学、设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: While they do more of other functions, there's still a lot of value, and tell me if I'm hearing you correct, in having this specific discipline and skill and background.
zh: 虽然他们做了更多其他职能的工作，但仍然有很多价值，如果我听得没错的话，拥有这种特定的纪律、技能和背景。

speaker: Unknown
en: I still see a craft excellence that's really important in the disciplines that I don't think is going away anytime soon, even if there's fluidity or blurring of the work across the functional lines.
zh: 我仍然看到一门技艺的卓越性，这在各个学科中非常重要，我认为这不会很快消失，即使工作在职能线条之间有流动性或模糊化。

speaker: Unknown
en: It goes back to what I mentioned earlier of you still have humans who have to make sure that what we're doing makes sense.
zh: 这回到了我之前提到的，我们仍然有人必须确保我们所做的是有道理的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're solving the right problems in a way that is best for Netflix members or business stakeholders.
zh: 我们正在以对 Netflix 成员或业务利益相关者最好的方式解决正确的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that if I talk to an engineer, a data scientist, a designer, yes, they speak more languages now than they used to because they have the benefit of these AI tools, but there's still something that is not replaceable when I think about the craft and how they think about what good looks like.
zh: 如果我与工程师、数据科学家、设计师交谈，是的，他们现在比过去使用更多的语言，因为他们有这些 AI 工具的好处，但当我想到这门手艺以及他们如何思考什么是好的时候，仍然有一些不可替代的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that feels true across all levels.
zh: 在所有层面上感觉都是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I still find great engineering to be scarce, great data science to be scarce, great creativity to be scarce.
zh: 而且我仍然发现伟大的工程是稀缺的，伟大的数据科学是稀缺的，伟大的创造力是稀缺的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, yes, some things are easier, but that hasn't dissolved in my mind.
zh: 所以，是的，有些事情变得更容易了，但这并没有在我脑海中消解。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are there functions that you are finding you are hiring more of, like the pie chart pie expanding, say for engineering or PM or design or something, and then functions you're need less of with AI tooling and LLMs rising?
zh: 是否有某些职能你发现你正在招聘更多的人，比如饼图饼在扩大，比如工程或 PM 或设计或其他东西，然后随着 AI 工具和 LLM 的兴起，你需要更少的职能？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not sure that it matches exactly to functions, but I can tell you what we're seeing more of, we need more of.
zh: 我不确定它是否完全对应于职能，但我可以告诉你我们看到的更多东西，我们需要更多的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need more systems thinkers in a world with AI.
zh: 在一个有 AI 的世界里，我们需要更多的系统思考者。

speaker: Unknown
en: That looks a little bit different across functions, but I could play out a couple examples.
zh: 这在各个职能部门看起来有点不同，但我可以举几个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, in our core infrastructure team at Netflix in central engineering, a lot of what made Netflix successful over time was that local teams with specific business problems could move fast to deliver.
zh: 所以，在 Netflix 的核心基础设施团队在中心工程中，长期以来 Netflix 成功的很大一部分原因是，具有特定业务问题的本地团队能够快速行动以交付。

speaker: Unknown
en: They very often were not feeling like they needed to be on a central paved path.
zh: 他们经常不觉得他们需要走在一条中心铺设的道路上。

speaker: Unknown
en: They built the stack that they needed to solve the problem and have the impact.
zh: 他们构建了解决问题所需的技术栈并产生影响力。

speaker: Unknown
en: In a world of AI with agents operating across multiple systems, wanting source of truth data, the importance of having preferred paved paths that get the most of the benefits and produce some guardrails so we can make sure we're doing good work.
zh: 在一个有 AI 的世界里，智能体在多个系统中运行，想要真实数据源，拥有优先铺设的道路的重要性，这些道路能获得最大的好处并产生一些护栏，以便我们可以确保我们正在做好的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Common infrastructure, common paved paths, solving problems once with a core set of capabilities becomes more important.
zh: 通用基础设施、通用的铺设路径，用一组核心能力一次性解决问题变得更加重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we are hiring more people who can look across all the business domains and abstract that to here's the building blocks we're going to need in a world with AI.
zh: 所以，我们正在招聘更多的人，他们可以查看所有业务领域并将其抽象为，这就是我们在一个有 AI 的世界里将需要的积木。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's one of the lenses, but also just with a lens of what got Netflix here doesn't get Netflix there.
zh: 所以，这是其中一个视角，但也是用一个视角来看，让 Netflix 走到这里的并不能让 Netflix 走到那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're going to have to have a stronger set of infrastructure to move quickly in this future.
zh: 我们将必须拥有一套更强大的基础设施，以便在这个未来快速行动。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that means that engineering profiles are more distributed systems, more infrastructure, more of that system thinking mindset than a local business expertise.
zh: 所以，这意味着工程师的侧写更多的是分布式系统，更多的是基础设施，更多的是那种系统思维的心态，而不是本土业务的专业知识。

speaker: Unknown
en: Though, of course, we still have people who are deep in personalization and advertising and content delivery.
zh: 虽然，当然，我们仍然有深耕个性化和广告以及内容交付的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it's more something additive for us to have that core infrastructure and systems thinking.
zh: 所以，对我们来说，拥有核心基础设施和系统思维是更具有一种 additive（附加）的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: If I take another example, like design, it's extremely important that our experienced design team is developing templates and again, systems thinking for what does great user design look like at Netflix, so that they can enable lots of people, including those who are not designers by training, to develop products that are coherent, that fit into the end-to-end member experience.
zh: 如果我再举一个例子，比如设计，非常有必要的是，我们经验丰富的设计团队正在开发模板，并且再次，系统思维，对于伟大的用户设计在 Netflix 看起来是什么样的，以便他们能够赋能很多人，包括那些没有受过设计训练的人，去开发连贯的产品，适合端到端会员体验的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I get really nervous about having different design languages or different types of user interactions and shipping Frankensteins, basically.
zh: 我对拥有不同的设计语言或不同类型的用户交互以及基本上拼凑出 Frankensteins（弗兰肯斯坦怪物）真的感到紧张。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, designers need to then be the people we're hiring again for design systems thinking.
zh: 所以，设计师必须成为我们要招聘的人，再次为了设计系统思维。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we think about templates and expression of the brand?
zh: 我们如何思考模板和品牌的表达？

speaker: Unknown
en: And what a good user experience looks like.
zh: 以及良好的用户体验看起来是什么样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what is Netflix and the Netflix differentiated special sauce?
zh: 以及什么是 Netflix 以及 Netflix 独特的 special sauce（秘制酱料）？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's more people on our design team that have to think that way now than, could I help to design a specific feature for a specific product?
zh: 所以，现在我们的设计团队里有更多人必须那样思考，而不是我可以帮助为特定产品设计特定功能？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's this stepping back to look at the big picture that I think is happening in every single function.
zh: 所以，有这种退后一步来看大局的思维，我认为这正在每一个单一的职能中发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that requires some reorientation of skills among the existing team and also hiring people who've got that type of expertise.
zh: 这需要对现有团队的技能进行一些重新定位，以及招聘拥有那种类型专业知识的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And across all of it, it's a mindset shift.
zh: 在所有这一切中，这是一种心态的转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we are not hiring people who are not excited to explore, try new things, understand lots is changing and feel comfortable with that ambiguity, be comfortable that there's a blurring of how we work and how we partner.
zh: 所以，我们不会招聘那些不兴奋去探索、尝试新事物、理解很多正在变化的事情并对那种模糊性感到舒服、对我们要如何工作和如何合作伙伴之间界限的模糊感到舒服的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's true for people who are already at Netflix and people who we are adding to the team, that that curiosity innovation mindset, it's not been more important, at least in the time that I've been working in this field.
zh: 对于已经在 Netflix 的人和我们要加入团队的人来说都是如此，那种好奇心创新的心态，这从来没有像现在这么重要，至少在我从事这个领域工作的这段时间里是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: On the systems thinking piece, is the reason this is becoming more important, that people are moving so fast that you need to invest in platforms and frameworks and design language, and basically teach people to fish so they can not be blocked or are there other reasons?
zh: 关于系统思维这一点，它变得如此重要的原因是因为人们移动得太快，所以你需要投资于平台和框架以及设计语言，并且基本上授人以渔，这样他们就不会被阻碍，还是有其他原因？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's probably velocity.
zh: 我认为可能是 velocity（速度）。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, platforms do have a benefit of leverage.
zh: 所以，平台确实有 leverage（杠杆）的好处。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, in general, that's an opportunity with or without AI, for a platform to get most teams 80% of the way there.
zh: 所以，一般来说，无论有没有 AI，这对平台来说都是一个机会，可以让大多数团队完成 80% 的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then they don't have to reinvent those building blocks.
zh: 然后他们就不必重新发明那些构建块。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have more bets that we're making across the business, more things we're trying to build.
zh: 我们在业务上进行了更多的押注，我们正在尝试构建更多的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, platform mindsets are good, and it's something that is relatively more recent for Netflix to think about that being a real critical enabler.
zh: 所以，平台心态是好的，这是 Netflix 相对较新的思维方式，认为这是一个真正的关键推动因素。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is also the sense of a scaffolding in a world of AI.
zh: 还有一种在 AI 世界中 scaffolding（脚手架）的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, not just the higher velocity, but you have more people doing more types of work that are different or new, like we were talking about.
zh: 所以，不仅是更高的速度，而且你有更多的人在做更多类型的、不同的或新的工作，就像我们刚才谈到的那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's risk that comes with, how do you think about access and identity in that situation?
zh: 并且伴随着风险，你会如何思考这种情况下的访问和身份？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you think about security in that situation?
zh: 你会如何思考这种情况下的安全性？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you think about shipping high quality code and design and user experiences?
zh: 你会如何思考交付高质量的代码和设计以及用户体验？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I don't think it scales well to have each person who's building something have to go figure out, could you remind me what good looks like here?
zh: 所以，我认为如果要扩展，让每个正在构建东西的人都必须去搞清楚，你能提醒我这里好的标准是什么吗，这是不能很好地扩展的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what are the bumpers or guardrails I should keep in mind?
zh: 以及我应该记住哪些 bumpers（缓冲带）或 guardrails（护栏）？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think we need to encode that in our paved paths and our ways of working.
zh: 我认为我们需要在我们的铺设路径和我们的工作方式中 encode（编码）这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: And for a data science or analytical field to encode, here's the source of truth data, here's how to interpret it, here's how to access it, here's what to do with it or not to do with it and to be careful with certain types of data.
zh: 对于数据科学或分析领域，要 encode（编码），这里是真实来源的数据，这里是如何解释它，这里是如何访问它，这里是用它做什么或不做什么，并且对某些类型的数据要小心。

speaker: Unknown
en: An organization that has thousands of people can no longer rely on tribal knowledge or I'm going to find the one person who knows this.
zh: 一个拥有数千人的组织不能再依赖 tribal knowledge（部落知识）或者我要去找到那个知道这个的一个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, this was a challenge that was there before AI.
zh: 所以，这是在 AI 之前就已经存在的挑战。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's probably a more urgent challenge with AI.
zh: 有了 AI，这可能是一个更紧迫的挑战。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I like the idea of using AI or any new tech to motivate... We knew this is work we needed to do.
zh: 我喜欢用 AI 或任何新技术来激励的想法……我们知道这是我们需要做的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: No time like the present to invest in that more heavily across the team.
zh: 没有比现在更好的时间来在整个团队中更深入地投资于此。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wonder if another reason for this becoming more valuable is because agents are now doing a lot of work.
zh: 我想知道这变得更有价值的另一个原因是否是因为 agents（智能体）现在正在做大量的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And giving them the context, giving them the scaffolding, giving them the design language, just speeds all that up?
zh: 并且给他们上下文，给他们 scaffolding（脚手架），给他们设计语言，只是加快了所有这一切？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one of the visions we have at Netflix is we will have so many agents that are contributing to doing work that you need to be able to reason and rationalize throughout that.
zh: 我们在 Netflix 的愿景之一是，我们将有这么多的 agents（智能体）在做工作方面做出贡献，你需要能够贯穿其中进行推理和合理化。

speaker: Unknown
en: The humans are the ones guiding what's the problem we need to solve.
zh: 人类是指导我们需要解决的问题的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do I feel like what we're producing is impactful and high quality output?
zh: 我是否觉得我们正在产出的是有影响力的和高质量的输出？

speaker: Unknown
en: But the work will be done by both humans and agents.
zh: 但工作将由人类和 agents（智能体）共同完成。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that creates velocity and benefits and it creates risks.
zh: 这创造了速度和好处，也创造了风险。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's important, especially from an engineering perspective, that we figure out how to manage that in a way that lets people move quickly, but doesn't create undue downside or risks for the company.
zh: 我认为这很重要，特别是从工程角度来看，我们要弄清楚如何管理它，以让人们快速行动，但不会给公司造成不当的下行风险或风险。

speaker: Unknown
en: This connects so directly with... Jenny Wen was on the podcast.
zh: 这直接联系到……Jenny Wen 曾在播客上。

speaker: Unknown
en: She was Head of Design for Claude Code and Cowork and had this whole design process is dead kind of thesis.
zh: 她是 Claude Code 和 Cowork 的设计负责人，并且有这整个设计过程已经死了的论点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the pitch there is just there's no time for design, the design process.
zh: 那里的观点只是没有时间做设计，设计过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And instead, as a designer, you're just kind of steering people and pointing them in the direction and adjusting and also thinking big pictures when you have the time.
zh: 相反，作为设计师，你只是在引导人们，为他们指明方向并进行调整，同时在有时间时思考大局。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it feels like that's what you're describing here is create the platform for people to move fast and then there's no time for design process of a specific new feature.
zh: 感觉你在这里描述的是为人们快速行动创建平台，然后就没有时间针对特定新功能进行设计过程了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have mixed feelings about that, because we do want to enable with infrastructure and systems thinking, more people to do great work with strong design as part of it.
zh: 我对此心情复杂，因为我们确实希望通过基础设施和系统思维，让更多人能够将强大的设计作为其工作的一部分来完成出色的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why not take that opportunity that the new tech provides?
zh: 为什么不利用新技术提供的机会呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: But for our most important priorities, design is critical to solve things in the right way.
zh: 但对于我们最重要的优先事项而言，对于以正确的方式解决问题，设计至关重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we do still make time for important design work.
zh: 所以，我们确实仍然会为重要的设计工作腾出时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: It can move faster.
zh: 它的进展可以更快。

speaker: Unknown
en: The designers themselves have more tools in their toolkit, so they can do incredible work at a faster velocity, show more options, learn, iterate, test more quickly.
zh: 设计师本身在他们的工具包中有更多的工具，所以他们可以以更快的速度完成令人难以置信的工作，展示更多的选项，学习，迭代，更快地测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think it would be a mistake to say design and deep design expertise and thinking gets squeezed out, just because we can write code faster, we can do data analysis faster.
zh: 但我认为如果说设计和深刻的设计专业知识及思维因此而被挤出，那将是一个错误，仅仅是因为我们可以更快地编写代码，我们可以更快地进行数据分析。

speaker: Unknown
en: At least for a large scale consumer product like Netflix, I feel like we would lose one of the things that makes Netflix great, which is the product technology and design makes a lot of complexity invisible and makes for a seamless customer experience.
zh: 至少对于像 Netflix 这样的大规模消费产品来说，我觉得我们会失去让 Netflix 变得伟大的东西之一，即产品和技术设计让很多复杂性变得不可见，从而带来了无缝的客户体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a design mindset that has to be core to it.
zh: 那是一种必须成为其核心的设计思维。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the work itself might look different, but I don't think we lose the mindset.
zh: 所以，工作本身可能看起来不同，但我不认为我们会失去这种思维。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's an awesome counterpoint.
zh: 这是一个很棒的反方观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, what I'm hearing is trending up skills, attributes you look for, systems thinking.
zh: 所以，我听到的是技能的提升，你寻找的特质，系统思维。

speaker: Unknown
