zh: 但这种影响是非常有希望的，我们正在看到很多制作利用不同的工具，其中一些是内部构建的，有些是我们通过其他供应商为那些内容创作用例提供的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then as we think about how content comes to the product, I mentioned localization, subtitles and dubs, but also how we create high quality trailers, images, artwork at scale that then we can use to help make sure that titles find their audiences around the world.
zh: 然后当我们考虑内容如何来到产品时，我提到了本地化、字幕和配音，还有我们如何规模化制作高质量的预告片、图片、艺术作品，然后我们可以用来帮助确保作品在世界范围内找到它们的受众。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those all are huge levers when we think about the AI impact.
zh: 当我们考虑 AI 影响时，这些都是巨大的杠杆。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that again, goes well beyond prototyping or coding to some of the creative use cases.
zh: 所以，这再次远远超出了原型制作或编码的范围，进入了某些创意用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can imagine that, just like they work for studio productions for film and TV, they work for advertising, they work for marketing, off-service campaigns.
zh: 你可以想象，就像它们为电影和电视的工作室制作服务一样，它们为广告服务，为营销服务，为站外活动服务。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, those are all areas that we're exploring.
zh: 所以，这些都是我们正在探索的领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by Mercury, radically different banking loved by over 300,000 entrepreneurs and now with Command.
zh: 本集由 Mercury 赞助播出，这是一种截然不同的银行业务，受到超过 30 万名创业者的喜爱，现在推出了 Command。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been a customer of Mercury's for over six years.
zh: 我成为 Mercury 的客户已经超过六年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have never once thought about leaving.
zh: 我从未想过要离开。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mercury is basically what happens when banking is built by product people, not by bankers.
zh: Mercury 基本上就是当银行业务是由产品人员而不是银行家构建时会发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: They make it so easy, dare I say fun to send invoices, move money around, set up virtual cards for folks on my team.
zh: 它们让它变得如此简单，我敢说甚至很有趣，比如发送发票、转移资金、为我的团队成员设置虚拟卡。

speaker: Unknown
en: Does your bank have an API, a terminal native CLI or an AI-ready MCP server?
zh: 你的银行有 API、终端原生 CLI 或支持 AI 的 MCP 服务器吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think so.
zh: 我认为没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just recently, they launched Command, a conversational interface built directly into Mercury, which acts as your financial operator.
zh: 就在最近，他们推出了 Command，这是一个直接构建在 Mercury 中的对话界面，充当你的财务操作员。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been using Command to transfer money around, to figure out what categories I've been spending the most money in, analyze my cash flows.
zh: 我一直使用 Command 来转移资金，弄清楚我在哪些类别上花钱最多，分析我的现金流。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just today, I used it to find out how much I've made from a specific sponsor over the past year.
zh: 就在今天，我用它来找出过去一年我从某个特定赞助商那里赚了多少钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just ask, how much have I made from X over the past year?
zh: 我只是问，过去一年我从 X 那里赚了多少钱？

speaker: Unknown
en: 10 seconds later I have an answer.
zh: 10 秒钟后我就得到了答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is so freaking cool.
zh: 这真是太酷了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Visit mercury.com to learn more and apply online in minutes.
zh: 访问 mercury.com 了解更多信息，并在几分钟内在线申请。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mercury is a FinTech company, not an FDIC-insured bank.
zh: Mercury 是一家金融科技公司，而不是 FDIC 承保的银行。

speaker: Unknown
en: Banking services provided through Choice Financial Group and Column NA Members FDIC.
zh: 银行服务通过 Choice Financial Group 和 Column NA 提供，成员 FDIC。

speaker: Unknown
en: You mentioned how Netflix has been very early to AI and ML for a long time.
zh: 你提到 Netflix 在 AI 和 ML 方面起步很早，并且已经很久了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Younger people may not remember this, but y'all had this contest to optimize the-
zh: 年轻人可能不记得这个，但你们举办过一场比赛来优化这个——

speaker: Unknown
en: The Netflix prize.
zh: Netflix 奖。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, the Netflix prize.
zh: 是的，Netflix 奖。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just show an example of how early you were to AI and ML.
zh: 就展示一个例子说明你们在 AI 和 ML 方面起步有多早。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it was a million dollar prize to optimize the Netflix ranking algorithm a little bit, whoever could optimize it the most.
zh: 我认为那是一百万美元的奖金，用来稍微优化一下 Netflix 排名算法，谁能优化得最多谁就赢。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the winner optimized it by a few percentage points, something like that.
zh: 我认为获胜者优化了几个百分点，大概就是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was a huge deal.
zh: 那是件大事。

speaker: Unknown
en: All these super smart people got around the world and it happened a few times, right?
zh: 世界各地所有的超级聪明人都聚集在一起，这发生过几次，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, you said it on my behalf.
zh: 我是说，你替我说出了我想说的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Often, when there's questions about how is Netflix thinking about AI, it's great to remind people of exactly that point, that this is not new to us.
zh: 通常，当有关于 Netflix 如何看待 AI 的问题时，提醒人们那一点是很棒的，即这对我们来说并不新鲜。

speaker: Unknown
en: That especially for personalization, it's been central to delivering a great experience to members.
zh: 特别是对于个性化推荐，它一直是为会员提供良好体验的核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's impossible to take the breadth of content that we have.
zh: 想要涵盖我们要拥有的内容的广度是不可能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's ever more content, that's one of the challenges we face.
zh: 内容越来越多，这是我们面临的挑战之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And make discovery easier and easier and easier, which is one of the challenges that Netflix has.
zh: 并且让发现变得越来越容易，这是 Netflix 面临的挑战之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And using AI and ML has been a way to do that.
zh: 使用 AI 和 ML 一直是做到这一点的一种方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: You want to personalize right title for the right person at the right moment.
zh: 你想在正确的时刻为正确的人个性化正确的标题。

speaker: Unknown
en: That problem gets harder.
zh: 这个问题变得更难了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The more exciting our catalog gets, the greater breadth of content we have, not just film and TV, but games and live and podcasts.
zh: 随着我们的目录变得更加令人兴奋，我们拥有更广度的内容，不仅仅是电影和电视，还有游戏、直播和播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Personalization becomes even more important and what that experience is.
zh: 个性化变得更加重要，而这就是那种体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we can take a lot of that history and say, okay, well, now how do we solve this problem?
zh: 所以，我们可以利用很多历史经验并说，好吧，现在我们要如何解决这个问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the tech is even more powerful, but it gives us a running head start in being clear about the problem to solve, how important it is that Netflix solve that for our members.
zh: 因为技术更强大了，但这给了我们一个领先的起跑线，明确要解决的问题，Netflix 为会员解决这个问题有多重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the same is true, as I was mentioning on the creative side of the house, AI and ML have been in things like visual effects or in localizing language for a long time.
zh: 而且同样的是，正如我提到的在创意领域，AI 和 ML 很久以来就一直用于视觉特效或语言本地化等方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now we say, what's the next era of that when the tech is more powerful?
zh: 现在我们要说，当技术更强大时，下一个时代是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And in both cases, it ends up taking a strength that Netflix has, which is marrying entertainment, and technology, and making sure we stay ahead of the game to deliver things that are even better.
zh: 在这两种情况下，最终都利用了 Netflix 的优势，即结合娱乐和技术，并确保我们保持领先地位，以交付更好的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I love that it's part of our history.
zh: 所以，我很喜欢它是我们历史的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: It still continues to be a strength and it's going to have to be a strength, given the size of the challenges we're facing around the breadth of entertainment, while keeping a great experience.
zh: 它仍然继续是一个优势，而且它必须是一个优势，鉴于我们在娱乐广度方面面临的挑战规模，同时还要保持良好的体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love that back then it was called machine learning and AI was like, no, no, it's not AI.
zh: 我很喜欢那时候它被称为机器学习，而 AI 就像是，不，不，它不是 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is never going to happen.
zh: AI 永远不会发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just machine learning.
zh: 这只是机器学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, then all of a sudden, we call everything AI and some of it's machine learning.
zh: 然后，突然之间，我们把一切都称为 AI，其中一些是机器学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: It depends, the thing that is of the moment to describe.
zh: 这取决于描述当下的时髦事物。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I think we bucket all of it as AI now.
zh: 所以，我认为我们现在把所有的这些都归类为 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's cool.
zh: 那很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's a lot of AI use cases that are not generative use cases.
zh: 有很多不是生成式用例的 AI 用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we could go down a deep dark hole of all the specific things.
zh: 所以，我们可以深入探讨所有具体的事情，掉进一个深坑里。

speaker: Unknown
en: But in general, I don't think it would surprise anyone that Netflix is using a broad array with so much excitement about what's possible.
zh: 但总的来说，我认为 Netflix 正在使用广泛的阵列，并对可能发生的事情感到如此兴奋，这不会让任何人感到惊讶。

speaker: Unknown
en: The fun thing at Netflix for the people who work here is that if you're really passionate about the applications of tech for creative outlets, for consumer products, for infrastructure, we have all of those problems and AI is at the center of them.
zh: 在 Netflix，对于在这里工作的人来说，有趣的是，如果你真的热衷于技术在创意出口、消费产品和基础设施方面的应用，我们有所有这些问题，而 AI 处于这些问题的中心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's good not to forget that that's true.
zh: 不要忘记这一点是好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if Netflix isn't branded as an AI company, AI is a tool that we're very comfortable using to get these great entertainment and technology outcomes.
zh: 即使 Netflix 没有被定位为一家 AI 公司，AI 也是我们非常乐意使用的工具，以获得这些伟大的娱乐和技术成果。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other really interesting thing, just to keep complimenting Netflix here, if you look at the early culture deck of Netflix and also our conversation last time, things that emerge from that are things like high agency.
zh: 另一个真正有趣的事情，这里继续赞扬 Netflix，如果你看看 Netflix 早期的文化幻灯片，以及我们上一次的对话，从中浮现出来的事情包括高智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: This was something core to Netflix in the beginning.
zh: 这是 Netflix 早期的核心内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: High agency, autonomy, high talent density, very bottoms-up thinking, super quick experiments and launching, paying top of market.
zh: 高智能体、自主权、高人才密度、非常自下而上的思维、超级快速的实验和发布、支付市场顶薪。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what I hear constantly now from how the top AI labs operate.
zh: 这就是我现在从顶级 AI 实验室的运作方式中不断听到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we're all ending here and this is where Netflix has been forever.
zh: 所以，我们都来到这里，而这正是 Netflix 一直以来的样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a little prescient in understanding what makes talent incredible.
zh: 它在理解是什么让人才变得不可思议方面具有一点先见之明。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've thought about all those aspects of the culture at Netflix as, this is going to sound a little bit nerdy, but excellence as an operating system.
zh: 我思考过 Netflix 文化中所有这些方面，这听起来会有点书呆子气，但就是卓越作为一种操作系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the goal of all those cultural elements wasn't the end bone themselves.
zh: 所以，所有这些文化元素的目标并不是它们本身作为最终目的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It wasn't, let's just make sure people have as much responsibility as possible.
zh: 它不是，让我们只确保人们拥有尽可能多的责任。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't like process, so let's make sure that we don't have any of that.
zh: 我们不喜欢流程，所以让我们确保我们没有任何流程。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was instead a very strongly held opinion that you get to excellence by giving people a lot of agency and accountability, by pushing decisions as deep in the organization as possible, hiring great people who can be trusted to have good judgment and make good decisions.
zh: 相反，这是一种非常强烈的观点，即通过赋予人们大量的自主权和问责制，通过将决策尽可能推向组织的深处，雇佣可以被信任拥有良好判断力和做出优秀决策的优秀人才，从而达到卓越。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that ends up driving incredible outcomes, plus a lot more motivation and sense of responsibility.
zh: 这最终会带来不可思议的成果，以及更多的动力和责任感。

speaker: Unknown
en: It means every person on the team can feel like I'm being given a lot of keys and a lot of accountability for what happens here.
zh: 这意味着团队中的每个人都可以感觉到，我被赋予了很多钥匙，并对这里发生的事情承担很多责任。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I myself feel like when you know you're carrying that level of trust and accountability, you want to do your best work.
zh: 而我自己觉得，当你知道你承担着这种程度的信任和责任时，你会想要做你最好的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, there's something that feels very intuitive about Netflix's culture has always been aiming at excellence.
zh: 所以，Netflix 的文化有一些感觉非常直观的东西，它一直以卓越为目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you have great talent and you give them the ability to do their best work without micromanaging it or drowning it in process, you actually get much better outcomes.
zh: 当你拥有优秀的人才，并赋予他们做最好的工作的能力，而不进行微观管理或让他们淹没在流程中时，你实际上会获得更好的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I do think that the newer era companies are picking up on something that is feeling very familiar to us.
zh: 所以，我确实认为新时代的公司正在注意到一些对我们来说非常熟悉的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's not something that comes easily.
zh: 这不是一件容易的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, culture's not a static thing.
zh: 所以，文化不是一成不变的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Culture needs to grow and evolve as the company gets bigger, the types of problems you're solving, change.
zh: 文化需要随着公司的壮大、你所解决的问题的类型的变化而成长和演变。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the notion that we're going for excellence and trusting that exceptional talent needs to be able to do their best work, that's unchanged and something that I think continues to be a special sauce for us.
zh: 但我们追求卓越以及相信优秀的人才需要能够做他们最好的工作这一观念是不变的，而且我认为这继续是我们的一个制胜法宝。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this concept.
zh: 我喜欢这个概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: Excellence as an operating system.
zh: 卓越作为一种操作系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very systems thinking you might say for how to set up a company.
zh: 你可以说这是关于如何建立一家公司的系统思维。

speaker: Unknown
en: Exactly, Lenny.
zh: 确实是这样，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, everyone listening to this will want excellence as an operating system.
zh: 所以，每个听到这个的人都希望卓越成为一种操作系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who would not want this?
zh: 谁不想要这个呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: It'd be helpful for people to hear what are the ingredients to make this happen.
zh: 如果人们能听到实现这一点的要素是什么，那会有所帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is obviously high talent density, just hiring only the best.
zh: 显然，第一是高人才密度，只雇佣最优秀的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two is accountability.
zh: 第二是问责制。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's the input and the output, essentially.
zh: 本质上，这里有输入和输出。

speaker: Unknown
en: Input, amazing people, the top people, make them accountable, give them autonomy.
zh: 输入，杰出的人，顶尖的人，让他们负责，给他们自主权。

speaker: Unknown
en: What would you say are the pillars of creating this excellence as an operating system, if founders are listening to this and like, I want them to do that?
zh: 如果创始人在听这个并且想，我也想那样做，你会说创造这种卓越作为操作系统的支柱是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, the talent density is the non-negotiable.
zh: 嗯，人才密度是不可妥协的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to start with that.
zh: 你必须从那里开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you don't have that, you can't get to a place where you have confidence in decision-making at all levels of the organization, allowing people to take risks and innovate quickly.
zh: 如果你没有那个，你就无法达到在组织各级拥有决策信心的地方，允许人们承担风险并快速创新。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a big part of excellence in the Netflix culture, which is being very comfortable with risk taking.
zh: 这是 Netflix 文化中卓越的重要组成部分，那就是对承担风险感到非常自在。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't try to avoid failures.
zh: 我们不试图避免失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: We try to recover quickly when we have them.
zh: 当我们有失败时，我们试图快速恢复。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's been great examples of that.
zh: 我认为这方面有很多很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our foray into live was a wonderful example of being comfortable taking a ton of risk, knowing it would be imperfect, knowing we would learn fast and we would be better for it.
zh: 我们涉足直播就是一个很好的例子，说明了对承担大量风险感到自在，知道它会不完美，知道我们会快速学习，并且我们会因此变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've never been prouder of the team, seeing how we worked through that.
zh: 看到我们如何度过难关，我从未为团队感到如此自豪。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you have to be talent density, comfortable that people are going to take the context that you give them, strong judgment, and risk-taking, and fight for the things that are the best outcomes for the business.
zh: 所以，你必须坚持人才密度，相信人们会利用你给他们的背景、强大的判断力和冒险精神，并为那些对业务最好的结果而奋斗。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to be very clear that what you're doing is driving outcomes for consumers and Netflix.
zh: 你必须非常清楚，你正在做的事情是为消费者和 Netflix 推动成果。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, Netflix matters, Netflix members' matter.
zh: 所以，Netflix 很重要，Netflix 的会员很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not about my own personal success or what I prefer.
zh: 这不是关于我个人自己的成功或我的偏好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's a selflessness that is part of this excellence operating system.
zh: 所以，有一种无私是这种卓越操作系统的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the other thing I would say is some of the things that are... they're really unnatural for humans to do.
zh: 然后我想说的另一件事是，有些事情……对人类来说真的很不自然。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I could give a couple examples of things to get comfortable with, which is there are certainly days where I see decisions happening and I think I would make a different decision.
zh: 所以，我可以举几个例子来说明需要适应的事情，当然有些日子我看到做出的决定，我会想我会做出不同的决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that really going to be the best thing?
zh: 那真的会是最好的事情吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: But my job, especially in the Netflix culture, is not to step in, in every one of those cases and overrule or veto or question someone.
zh: 但我的工作，特别是在 Netflix 文化中，并不是在每一种情况下都介入，去推翻或否决或质疑某人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Especially if it's not material, it's not going to burn the place down, let people make that decision and learn from it and ask for those reflections afterwards of how did it go.
zh: 特别是如果它不重要，它不会把地方烧毁，让人们做那个决定并从中学习，并在事后询问那些反思，进行得如何。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe I was wrong.
zh: 也许我错了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe the decision was a great one.
zh: 也许那个决定很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that it's related to the risk taking.
zh: 但这与承担风险有关。

speaker: Unknown
en: And help people learn how to feel comfortable making their own decisions, especially when they're not all going to be the right decisions and they're going to learn something tough from it.
zh: 并帮助人们学会如何对做出自己的决定感到自在，尤其是当这些决定并不都会是正确的决定，并且他们将从中学到艰难教训的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: I felt that myself from my boss and my peers, saying, "This is your decision.
zh: 我从我的老板和我的同僚那里感受到了这一点，他们说，“这是你的决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can provide input.
zh: 我可以提供意见。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can help you brainstorm.
zh: 我可以帮你进行头脑风暴。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's yours in the end."
zh: 归根结底这是你的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it just doesn't come naturally.
zh: 而这并非天生如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: When the stakes are high, when I feel responsible for what the org's doing to let people lean into risk, can be uncomfortable.
zh: 当风险很高时，当我觉得要对组织做的事情负责以让人们倾向于承担风险时，可能会让人感到不舒服。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that also means in cases where things are not going well, as another example, to not assume that process is going to fix it.
zh: 而且我认为这也意味着在事情进展不顺利的情况下，作为另一个例子，不要假设流程能解决它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something I've learned over the past few years, that when planning is difficult, I've never heard someone say like, "Oh, we figured out the perfect way to plan or the perfect way to go through feedback and leveling and compensation."
zh: 我在过去几年中学到的一件事是，当规划很难时，我从没听人说过，“噢，我们找到了规划的完美方式，或者通过反馈和定级以及薪酬的完美方式。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But every time we saw that and we added more process, we spent more time without getting better outcomes.
zh: 但每次我们看到这种情况并增加更多流程时，我们花了更多时间却没有得到更好的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, it's another unnatural thing that I think everyone's inclination when things are hard and complicated, is you think you're simplifying the problem by putting a lot of constraints around it.
zh: 所以，这是另一件不自然的事情，我认为当事情艰难和复杂时，每个人的倾向是你认为通过给问题施加很多约束，你在简化问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it actually goes against the, is there a more creative way to plan or to make people decisions or to make prioritization decisions that actually get us to better outcomes?
zh: 但这实际上违背了，是否有更有创意的方式来规划，或者做出人员决策，或者做出优先级决策，从而真正让我们获得更好的结果？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, it's a resistance to do the thing that a lot of bigger companies would do and to feel comfortable in that discomfort very often.
zh: 所以，这是一种抵制，去不做许多大公司会做的事情，并且经常在那种不适中感到自在。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's something I feel in my role and I would believe a lot of people at Netflix feel it, because you try not to do the thing that is standard.
zh: 所以，这是我在我的角色中感受到的，我相信 Netflix 的许多人也有同感，因为你试图不做那些标准的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's easy to say that and hear that, but I so know what you mean, where somebody screws up and you're like, "Okay, what was the thing that went wrong?
zh: 说起来和听起来都很容易，但我太明白你的意思了，就是当有人搞砸了，你会说，“好吧，出问题的是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's put a process in place to avoid this from happening."
zh: 让我们制定一个流程来避免这种情况再次发生。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And what you're saying is you need to resist that, because that slows things down.
zh: 而你说的是你需要抵制那种冲动，因为那会拖慢事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the best people don't want to be working in a place with all these checklists and processing gates and things like that.
zh: 而且最优秀的人不想在一个充满所有这些清单和处理关卡之类东西的地方工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the best people want to know there's going to be a blameless retro and they're going to feel so individually responsible that they're going to say, "How do I make sure this doesn't happen again?"
zh: 我认为最优秀的人想知道将会是一次无责复盘，而且他们会感到如此 individually responsible，以至于他们会说，“我要如何确保这不再发生？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Not with process, but with how could I share these learnings?
zh: 不是通过流程，而是通过我如何分享这些经验教训？

speaker: Unknown
en: How could I do work differently to make sure that I get to a better outcome next time?
zh: 我如何以不同的方式工作，以确保下次我获得更好的结果？

speaker: Unknown
en: When you are trusting people to take those reflections and learn and grow, I think you get much better outcomes over time.
zh: 当你信任人们去进行这些反思、学习和成长时，我认为随着时间的推移你会获得更好的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you get a much stronger team, which I think is part of our role as leaders, of you're trying to grow a team that is resilient and durable and knows how to have great impact.
zh: 而且你会得到一个更强大的团队，我认为这是我们作为领导者角色的一部分，你在试图培养一个有韧性、持久耐用且知道如何产生巨大影响的团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not trying to control everything.
zh: 你不是试图控制一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is a key to building a team with high talent density.
zh: 这是建立一个具有高人才密度团队的关键。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's two sides of this that I want to chat about briefly.
zh: 关于这一点，我想简要聊两个方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is the hiring and the other is keeping the people.
zh: 一个是招聘，另一个是留住人才。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you're famous for the keeper's test.
zh: 所以，你以 keeper's test（留任测试）而闻名。

speaker: Unknown
en: We talked about this last time.
zh: 我们上次谈过这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another unnatural thing for people.
zh: 对人们来说另一件不自然的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: People that want to understand what this is, they can listen to the first conversation, but how has that evolved over the last couple of years?
zh: 想要了解这是什么的人，可以听第一次的对话，但这在过去几年里是如何演变的？

speaker: Unknown
en: That still a core part of the culture, this idea of the keeper's test?
zh: keeper's test 这个理念仍然是文化的核心部分吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's often cited in a way where you think of keeper's test as that moment where you decide to let someone go, that they're not the right fit for the role and the conversation about that.
zh: 人们经常以一种引用方式来思考 keeper's test，认为它是你决定让某人离开的那一刻，他们不适合这个角色以及关于此的对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's equally commonly used to have a conversation about how extraordinary someone is, how well they're doing in a role.
zh: 但它同样常被用来进行关于某人是多么非凡、他们在某个角色中做得多么好的对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the entry point is for me to say to one of my direct reports or for them to say to me, "How am I doing on your keeper test?"
zh: 因为切入点是让我对我的直接下属之一说，或者让他们对我说，“我在你的 keeper's test 中表现如何？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the lion's share of the time, my response is, "I would fight so hard to keep you.
zh: 在绝大部分时间里，我的回答是，“我会非常努力地留住你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me go through a set of things that I think you're doing such a great job at, what your strengths are, where you're having a lot of impact.
zh: 让我列举一些我认为你做得非常出色的事情，你的优势是什么，你在哪里产生了很多影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's how you could be even better."
zh: 这里是你如何可以做得更好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it's an entry into a conversation that is very positive and uplifting for people, but the framing is, do I pass the keeper test?
zh: 所以，这是进入一个非常积极和令人振奋的对话的切入点，但框架是，我通过 keeper's test 了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, of course, there's the harder situations where I'm evaluating, does someone pass the keeper test or they're asking me?
zh: 然后，当然，还有更困难的情况，我在评估某人是否通过了 keeper's test 或者他们在问我？

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is the toughest thing to say, "To be honest, you're not passing that right now.
zh: 这是最难说出口的事情，“老实说，你现在没有通过。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you could get there in some cases."
zh: 我认为在某些情况下你可以达到那个标准。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that comes with feedback and what are those milestones.
zh: 这伴随着反馈以及那些里程碑是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or in some cases you're saying, "We've really tried and I don't see the path to success."
zh: 或者在某些情况下你会说，“我们真的尝试过了，但我看不到成功的路径。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it's an anchor and an entry point for a conversation that can go lots of different directions.
zh: 所以，它是一个对话的锚点和切入点，可以朝很多不同的方向发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the thing I like about it is it's good hygiene on feedback and checking in on how things are going and forcing a tough conversation sometimes, instead of shying away from it.
zh: 我喜欢它的地方在于，它是关于反馈和检查事情进展的良好卫生习惯，并有时强制进行艰难的对话，而不是回避它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or to keep great talent, you do need to say you're doing great.
zh: 或者为了留住优秀人才，你确实需要说你做得很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's an important part of making people feel recognized and valued.
zh: 这是让人感到被认可和重视的重要组成部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I don't want it to come across that we just have this very negative view of it.
zh: 所以，我不希望它给人的印象是我们对它有着非常消极的看法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's this positive side of the coin as well.
zh: 我想这也有硬币积极的一面。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I guess just to explain to people what this is so they don't have to go listen to a whole other podcast, I'll try to briefly explain it.
zh: 我想只是向人们解释这是什么，这样他们就不必去听另一整期播客，我会尝试简要解释一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: The idea here, a part of the Netflix culture is that when you have people reporting to you, you should always be thinking, would I hire this person today knowing what I know about them?
zh: 这里的想法是，Netflix 文化的一部分是，当你有向你汇报的人时，你应该总是在想，以我对他们的了解，我今天会雇佣这个人吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And if not, then I should probably let them go.
zh: 如果不，那么我可能应该让他们离开。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the idea there is to keep the high bar, to not ever just settle.
zh: 那里的想法是保持高标准，永远不要只是凑合。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, this person, they're here, I guess we'll keep them around.
zh: 好吧，这个人，他们在这里，我想我们会留他们在身边。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that roughly the way to understand it?
zh: 这大概就是理解它的方式吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the way it can... it's sort of a corollary to that.
zh: 它可以……的方式……这算是那一点的一个推论。

speaker: Unknown
en: If that person came to me today to say they were leaving, would I fight to keep them or not?
zh: 如果那个人今天来跟我说他们要离开，我会争取留住他们吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or would I say, if my sense is relief of, oh, yeah, it probably would be better to have someone else in this role, I should've taken action and having that conversation sooner.
zh: 还是说，如果我的感觉是松了一口气，哦，是的，在这个角色上有个其他人可能会更好，我应该早点采取行动并进行那次谈话。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you said, it's so many uncomfortable things you have to do to maintain this culture.
zh: 正如你所说，为了维持这种文化，你必须做这么多不舒服的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, the keeper test is one, maintaining talent density, context, not control among leaders.
zh: 好吧，看守测试是其中之一，维持人才密度，领导者之间的情境而非控制。

speaker: Unknown
en: We talk about being highly aligned, but loosely coupled, which is where light process, the minimum to make sure we're clear on the priorities and we can execute them is what we're solving for.
zh: 我们谈论高度一致，但松散耦合，这意味着轻量级流程，这是确保我们对优先事项清晰并能够执行它们的最低限度，这就是我们要解决的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: All of these things are not things that human beings or organizations at scale tend to do.
zh: 所有这些都不是人类或大规模组织倾向于做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it's constant diligence to try to maintain the thing that's made Netflix a special place.
zh: 所以，这需要不懈的勤奋来试图维持让 Netflix 成为一个特别之地的因素。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because in the end, it's the work and the culture that attracts people and retains people.
zh: 因为归根结底，是工作和文化在吸引人并留住人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we need that to be a successful business.
zh: 我们需要这样才能成为一家成功的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's exactly where I was going to go.
zh: 这正是我想要去的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, to make this work, you need to attract the best people.
zh: 所以，为了实现这一点，你需要吸引最优秀的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's always been very hard to attract the best people.
zh: 吸引最优秀的人一直很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels insanely hard these days with the amount of dollars flying around, the fancy AI labs, so much competition.
zh: 如今，随着大量资金流动，花哨的 AI 实验室，以及如此激烈的竞争，感觉难如登天。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's crazy.
zh: 这太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: What have you found to be effective in convincing the top people to still come to Netflix and join versus all the other fancy places they can go?
zh: 你发现什么有效的方法来说服顶尖人才依然加入 Netflix，而不是去他们能去的所有其他花哨的地方？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've always had a lot of competition for talent.
zh: 我们在人才方面一直面临很多竞争。

speaker: Unknown
en: It might feel more pronounced right now, but we have great talent on the team.
zh: 现在可能感觉更明显了，但我们团队有优秀的人才。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe that goes without saying, but I feel like I should say it out loud because I believe it.
zh: 也许这是不言而喻的，但我觉得我应该大声说出来，因为我相信它。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have incredible talent at Netflix, recent hires, long tenured people.
zh: 我们在 Netflix 有令人难以置信的人才，新员工，长期任职的员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm always impressed by the work that the team is doing.
zh: 我总是对团队正在做的工作印象深刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I don't feel like we've suffered or other companies are vacuuming up all the good people, because so many of them I do think sit at Netflix.
zh: 所以，我不觉得我们受了苦，或者其他公司正在吸走所有的好人，因为我认为他们中的很多人都坐在 Netflix。

speaker: Unknown
en: It does feel like we have to be more explicit about the types of people and talent that tend to thrive at Netflix versus other companies, like some of the Frontier Labs.
zh: 感觉确实我们需要更明确地说明在 Netflix 与其他公司（比如一些 Frontier Labs）倾向于蓬勃发展的人才类型。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, people at Netflix have to be passionate about the application of technology and the application or building products to solve a certain set of problems.
zh: 所以，Netflix 的人必须对技术的应用以及利用或构建产品来解决某些特定问题的充满热情。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to love entertainment.
zh: 你必须热爱娱乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to love consumer products at scale.
zh: 你必须热爱大规模的消费品。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to love the global nature of that.
zh: 你必须热爱这种全球性质。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are a lot of incredibly talented people who love that sweet spot, I am one of them, between tech and product and entertainment.
zh: 有非常多才华横溢的人热爱那个甜蜜点，在技术和产品以及娱乐之间，我也是其中之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And how do you make those things come together in a way that's remarkable?
zh: 以及你如何以一种非凡的方式让这些事情走到一起？

speaker: Unknown
en: And you use AI to do it.
zh: 而且你使用 AI 来做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: You use other technologies and products to do it, but that has to be something that drives you to be really excited about a lot of the roles at Netflix.
zh: 你使用其他技术和产品来做到这一点，但这必须是某种驱动你对 Netflix 的许多角色感到真正兴奋的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: If instead you're inspired by some of the foundational work that the frontier model companies are doing, which is exciting in its own way, it's a different persona.
zh: 相反，如果你受到前沿模型公司正在做的一些基础工作的启发，这本身令人兴奋，但那是不同的个性/人物画像。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a different...
zh: 那是不同的……

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's the problem space that I want to work in.
zh: 这就是我想从事的问题空间。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I don't think there's a shortage of people who get really excited about the applications of the technology and see the connection to that, to things that they love and use every day, like Netflix.
zh: 但我不缺那些对技术的应用真正感到兴奋并看到与之联系的人，看到他们每天喜爱和使用的东西之间的联系，比如 Netflix。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, that gets me up in the morning and I think it gets a lot of the team members up.
zh: 所以，这让我每天早起，我想这也让许多团队成员早起。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we have this conversation about that's something special that only talent at Netflix can do, or fill in the blank for another industry that's deep in the application of it.
zh: 我们有过这样的谈话，说这是只有 Netflix 的人才才能做到的特殊事情，或者填补另一个在应用方面很深的行业的空白。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's inspiring.
zh: 我认为这很鼓舞人心。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to touch on a couple things that I've been thinking about in this world of AI that we're approaching.
zh: 我想谈谈在这个我们正在接近的 AI 世界中我一直在思考的几件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is junior people.
zh: 一个是初级人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels like everyone's just like...
zh: 感觉每个人都只是……

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a good example.
zh: 这是一个很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're hiring a lot of awesome senior people that have proven they're awesome and high talent, density, high bars.
zh: 你在招聘很多很棒的资深人员，他们已经证明了自己很棒，且具有高人才密度、高标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, just AI makes it so easy to do stuff that people may not be learning how to do anything.
zh: 另外，仅仅是 AI 让做事变得如此容易，以至于人们可能没有在学习如何做任何事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like junior engineers, I'm thinking, or junior PMs, junior designers.
zh: 比如初级工程师，我在想，或者初级 PM、初级设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do new people become these awesome senior people?
zh: 新人如何成为这些很棒的资深人员？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything you think about?
zh: 有什么你考虑到的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you hiring junior people?
zh: 你们在招初级人员吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you think about this?
zh: 你怎么看这个问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: What happens with junior people not necessarily learning or having a path to learn to become the senior person?
zh: 如果初级人员不一定在学习或没有途径去学习成为资深人员，会发生什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: We are still hiring junior people and they're really important to our talent strategy.
zh: 我们仍在招聘初级人员，他们对我们的人才战略真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we still have an intern program, we still have a new grad program, which was new for us as of a few years ago.
zh: 所以，我们仍然有实习项目，我们仍然有应届毕业生项目，这对我们来说是几年前才有的新东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, prior to a few years ago, we were only hiring more experienced talent across all the functions.
zh: 所以，在几年前之前，我们在所有职能部门只招聘更有经验的人才。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now we do hire people straight from undergrad and graduate programs and we'll continue to do that.
zh: 现在我们确实直接从本科和研究生项目中招聘人员，我们将继续这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, even in a world of AI where some things are easier, we were talking earlier about mindset, AI fluency.
zh: 所以，即使在某些事情变得更容易的 AI 世界里，我们之前也谈到了心态、AI 熟练度。

speaker: Unknown
en: From my experience, younger folks are more open-minded.
zh: 根据我的经验，年轻人更思想开明。

speaker: Unknown
en: They tend to be more native in some of these new ways of working.
zh: 他们对这些新的工作方式往往更原生。

speaker: Unknown
en: For a company like Netflix, they're also very fluent in how entertainment is changing, how consumer behaviors are changing, how product and tech is influencing that in the products that they're using.
zh: 对于像 Netflix 这样的公司，他们对娱乐的变化方式、消费者行为的变化方式、产品和技术如何影响他们正在使用的产品也非常熟练。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's really important to have on our team.
zh: 拥有这些对我们的团队来说真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's the part of the persona, which is who are you as a new grad who's an engineer?
zh: 所以，这是角色形象的一部分，即作为一名工程师的应届毕业生你是谁？

speaker: Unknown
en: But there's also, who are you as someone who's in their early twenties and has a perspective on the world that is highly valuable and a comfort with the way the world is changing?
zh: 但也有，作为一个二十出头的人你是谁，你对世界的观点具有很高的价值，并且对世界的变化方式感到适应？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's why I say it's a critical part of our talent strategy.
zh: 所以，这就是为什么我说它是我们人才战略的关键部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: To the, okay, so you step into the role and you have AI tools that didn't exist five or 10 years ago.
zh: 至于，好吧，当你进入这个角色，你拥有 5 年或 10 年前不存在的 AI 工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say mastery of the craft is still very important.
zh: 我想说的是，技艺的掌握仍然非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, going back to, as the team member, I'm responsible for the quality of code that I am submitting for production.
zh: 所以，回到作为团队成员，我对我提交用于生产的代码质量负责。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm responsible for the quality of products that I'm building, how they are designed, what that user-consumer experience is.
zh: 我对我构建的产品质量、它们的设计方式、用户体验是什么负责。

speaker: Unknown
en: None of that is going away.
zh: 这些都不会消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, if I think about more junior or earlier career talent on the teams, we need to be investing just as much in the mentorship of this is what good looks like.
zh: 所以，如果我在团队中想到更初级或职业生涯早期的人才，我们需要在这些方面的指导上投入同样多的精力：什么是好的标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is how you use these tools, but you still take accountability for what the outcomes are, what the quality of the output is.
zh: 这就是你使用这些工具的方式，但你仍然要对结果是什么、输出的质量是什么负责。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think I mentioned this earlier, I find that mastery and that craft excellence scarce still.
zh: 我想我之前提到过这一点，我发现这种掌握和技艺卓越仍然稀缺。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we want to make sure we're teaching that.
zh: 所以，我们要确保我们在教授这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's a valid concern of, how do I get that if I'm not as hands-on as I would've had to be?
zh: 我认为这是一个合理的担忧，如果我不像我本来必须那样亲自动手，我该如何获得那种能力？

speaker: Unknown
en: But you still carry responsibility for reviewing code, testing code, being able to diagnose problems, knowing what a good product looks like.
zh: 但你仍然承担着审查代码、测试代码、能够诊断问题、知道什么是好产品的责任。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's a very scarce skill to say, this is excellence in a product that solves a problem that matters and in how it's designed.
zh: 我认为这是一种非常稀缺的技能，即说出这是在一个设计良好的产品中的卓越，它解决了一个重要的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I don't think that craft mastery, the importance of it is going away.
zh: 所以，我不认为这种技艺掌握、它的重要性会消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: Probably the way we train and grow talent has to change, because they're going to use different tools.
zh: 可能我们培训和培养人才的方式必须改变，因为他们将使用不同的工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I can guarantee you that earlier career talent is going to be teaching older folks like me many new things, too.
zh: 我可以向你保证，职业生涯早期的人才也会教像我这样的年长者很多新东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I think it goes in both directions.
zh: 所以，我认为这是双向的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where do you think engineering goes in, I don't know, five, 10 years?
zh: 你认为工程在五年、十年后会走向何方？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think people need to still understand code?
zh: 你认为人们还需要理解代码吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or do you think there's this abstraction layer that sits on top where you don't even have to learn C++, Java, Python, whatever?
zh: 还是你认为有一个抽象层位于顶部，你甚至不需要学习 C++、Java、Python 等等？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's a difference between being able to write lines of code in a particular language, like Python or C++ and understanding how code, computer systems, products work.
zh: 我认为能够用特定的语言（如 Python 或 C++）写代码行与理解代码、计算机系统、产品如何工作之间是有区别的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't think the latter is going away, because if we trusted agents to know all the languages and write all the code, we're not going to know why is something... is it a good product?
zh: 我不认为后者会消失，因为如果我们信任智能体去了解所有语言并编写所有代码，我们将不会知道为什么某东西……它是好产品吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it a bad product?
zh: 它是坏产品吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it working as we expected when it doesn't?
zh: 当它不按预期工作时是否按预期工作？

speaker: Unknown
en: Like I mentioned earlier, we take a lot of risk.
zh: 就像我之前提到的，我们承担很多风险。

speaker: Unknown
en: We fail fast, we recover fast.
zh: 我们失败得快，恢复得快。

speaker: Unknown
en: That requires an understanding of how are these systems working.
zh: 这需要理解这些系统是如何工作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I might use an agent to help me understand those things, help me detect an anomaly or something that's broken faster and triage it, but I still need to have a fluency of what is this thing that we're building and how does it work, so I know if it's good and I know how to fix it.
zh: 我可能会使用智能体来帮助我理解这些事情，帮助我更快地检测异常或损坏的东西并进行分类，但我仍然需要对我们正在构建的这个东西是什么以及它如何工作有熟练度，所以我知道它是否是好的，并且我知道如何修复它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hope that doesn't go away, because that's like a, how do we make the world a better place through the stuff that we're building?
zh: 我希望那不会消失，因为那就像是，我们要如何通过我们正在构建的东西让世界变得更美好？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think requires some understanding of what we've built.
zh: 我认为这需要对我们构建的东西有一些理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I'm hearing, which it makes sense, is you may not have to write the code, but you have to understand it and what's happening.
zh: 我听到的意思是，这很合理，你可能不需要编写代码，但你必须理解它以及正在发生什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's so much harder to just... as a person not writing it to actually have that instilled in you.
zh: 但对于一个不编写代码的人来说，要将这种理解真正灌输给自己要难得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's one of the things that the learning curve is very steep on right now.
zh: 我认为这是目前学习曲线非常陡峭的事情之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, looking at some of the code that some of these models or agents are writing, they're very hard to follow.
zh: 所以，看看其中一些模型或智能体正在编写的一些代码，它们很难看懂。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, I know I'm getting better performance from this, but I have no idea why.
zh: 就像是，我知道我从中获得了更好的性能，但我不知道为什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if this thing breaks, I'm going to have no idea how to fix it.
zh: 如果这个东西坏了，我将完全不知道如何修复它。

speaker: Unknown
en: That makes me uncomfortable.
zh: 这让我感到不舒服。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe that's because I'm still on that learning curve of, how do we operate in that world?
zh: 也许这是因为我仍然处于那个学习曲线上，即我们如何在这个世界中运作？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the set of tests or rationalization and understanding that we need to have to get comfortable with it?
zh: 我们需要什么样的测试集、理性理解和理解才能对它感到舒适？

speaker: Unknown
en: But at first glance, it looks very unfamiliar and very unsettling.
zh: 但乍一看，它看起来非常陌生，非常令人不安。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I think engineering over time will evolve to be comfortable with that, and have fluency in it, and know how to guide new tech, and agents, and new capabilities to make sure that we feel really good about what the output is.
zh: 所以，我认为工程学随着时间的推移会演变，以适应这一点，并对其流利，知道如何引导新技术和智能体，以及新能力，以确保我们对输出感觉真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wonder what the metaphor is for this where this...
zh: 我想知道这里的隐喻是什么，这里这个……

speaker: Unknown
en: I continue to be astounded by how much engineering has transformed in two years.
zh: 工程学在两年内发生了多大的转变，我继续感到震惊。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like a completely different dropdown.
zh: 这就像是一个完全不同的下拉菜单。

speaker: Unknown
en: We used to sit there in NID and write code, and now you're just talking to agents and reviewing code and shipping 100 PRs a day.
zh: 我们过去常常坐在 NID 里编写代码，现在你只是在和智能体交谈，审查代码，并且每天运送 100 个 PR。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels like it's an acceleration of how much engineering has changed.
zh: 感觉就像是工程学变化速度的一种加速。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you looked over the last 10 years or 20 years, you would say the same thing.
zh: 但如果你看看过去 10 年或 20 年，你会说出同样的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's just something that's moving faster.
zh: 所以，只是有些东西移动得更快了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's hard to wrap our heads around how quickly it's moved in the past couple of years.
zh: 我们很难理解过去几年它移动得有多快。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's not totally unfamiliar that engineering or data science or product would have these big shifts, just like how filmmaking works.
zh: 但工程学、数据科学或产品发生这些巨大转变并不是完全陌生的，就像电影制作是如何运作的一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you look over the last 100 years, it's unbelievably different because of technology and new tools that we've brought to it.
zh: 如果你看看过去 100 年，它是令人难以置信地不同，因为我们引入的技术和工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just feels like the cycle is speeding up.
zh: 只是感觉周期正在加速。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to talk about entertainment for a brief moment.
zh: 我想简要地谈谈娱乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm curious just how entertainment will change over time in the next, I don't know, five, 10 years.
zh: 我只是好奇娱乐在未来，我不知道，五到十年内会如何变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just today, we open up Netflix, check out some shows, watch some videos, it hasn't changed in a while.
zh: 就在今天，我们打开 Netflix，查看一些节目，观看一些视频，它已经有一段时间没有改变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just that idea of like, oh, I'm going to watch The Pitt and just watch it all.
zh: 只是那个想法像是，哦，我要去看《The Pitt》然后一口气看完。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to watch a movie.
zh: 我要去看一部电影。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got TikTok, I got Instagram, feeds of stuff.
zh: 我有 TikTok，我有 Instagram，各种东西的信息流。

speaker: Unknown
en: How much different do you think this will be in, I don't know, five years, the way we entertain ourselves?
zh: 你认为这在多大程度上会有所不同，我不知道，五年后，我们娱乐自己的方式？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's already changing at Netflix, because entertainment is not going to be one thing in the future and it's already not one thing now.
zh: 我认为它已经在 Netflix 发生了变化，因为娱乐在未来不会是一件事，而且现在它也已经不是一件事了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, part of the reason that we are going beyond film and TV in our offering is because there's an expectation that consumers have of much greater variety across formats, devices, moments of the day, that Netflix needs to be able to serve well in order to meet consumer expectations and hopefully exceed them over time.
zh: 所以，我们要超越影视节目的原因之一是，消费者对跨格式、设备、一天中的时刻有更多样化的期望，Netflix 需要能够很好地服务这些期望，以满足消费者期望，并希望随着时间的推移超越它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, when we think about the addition of mobile and TV or cloud games, live content, podcasts, working with a broader set of creators who are now on the Netflix service, all of those things create a greater breadth of what entertainment is.
zh: 所以，当我们考虑添加移动端和电视端或云游戏、直播内容、播客，以及与现在 Netflix 服务上更广泛的创作者合作时，所有这些都创造了娱乐是什么的更广度。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Netflix is able to define and expand that.
zh: 而且 Netflix 能够定义并扩展这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it puts a higher bar or expectation on, how do we make sense of that for a Netflix member?
zh: 这对我们来说如何为 Netflix 会员理清这一点设定了更高的标准或期望？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, how do we show you this very seamless journey from I listened to the Bill Simmons podcast to I watch Quarterback, because I love that as one of the Netflix offerings in the more, you could say traditional film or TV space to I play the most recent FIFA cloud game.
zh: 那么，我们如何向你展示这条非常无缝的旅程，从我听 Bill Simmons 播客到我观看《Quarterback》，因为我喜欢这是 Netflix 提供内容之一，在更传统的影视空间中，到我玩最新的 FIFA 云游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I want to be able to do that in both TV and on my mobile phone, because now I'm on the move and I want to be able to discover and engage with the content at different moments of the day.
zh: 我希望能够在电视和我的手机上做到这一点，因为现在我在移动中，我希望能够在一天中的不同时刻发现并参与内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's already a journey that we're building into Netflix, which I think will become stronger and stronger over time.
zh: 这已经是我们要构建到 Netflix 中的旅程，我认为随着时间的推移会变得越来越强。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the future of entertainment isn't going to be one thing.
zh: 所以，娱乐的未来不会是一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's going to have to be more personalized, more immersive, more interactive, with this sense of this is a world that I can explore in lots of different directions, depending on what I'm looking for in the moment.
zh: 它必须更加个性化，更加沉浸式，更加互动，有一种这是一个我可以朝很多不同方向探索的世界的感觉，取决于我此刻在寻找什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the challenge Netflix has is we've got to make discovery and engagement much easier than it feels today.
zh: Netflix 面临的挑战是，我们必须让发现和参与比今天感觉到的更容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have tons of content, it can feel very fragmented, especially when you consider all the services or offerings out there.
zh: 我们有海量的内容，它可能会感觉非常碎片化，尤其是当你考虑到所有服务或产品时。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think Netflix is very well-positioned to understand how to solve that problem across entertainment, product and tech.
zh: 我认为 Netflix 非常有能力理解如何跨娱乐、产品和技术解决这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other element of this is AI, obviously.
zh: 另一个要素是 AI，显然。

speaker: Unknown
en: As an outside observer, it's so interesting to see how in tech it's like AI, I love it.
zh: 作为一个外部观察者，看看在科技界它是像 AI，我爱它，这太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the future, it's the best.
zh: 它是未来，它是最好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: In Hollywood it's like, no, shut it down.
zh: 在好莱坞则是，不，把它关掉。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a mix.
zh: 混合的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a very wide array.
zh: 范围非常广。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, Netflix's role in this is to enable creators with whatever tools they want to use to bring their vision to life.
zh: 所以，Netflix 在这方面的作用是让创作者能够使用他们想使用的任何工具来实现他们的愿景。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are going to be some creators or filmmakers who are on the end of the spectrum that says, "Absolutely not.
zh: 会有一些创作者或电影制作人处于光谱的一端，他们说，“绝对不行。

speaker: Unknown
en: No AI.
zh: 不要 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is not how I do production.
zh: 我不是这么搞制作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not consistent with my vision."
zh: 这与我的愿景不一致。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's fine.
zh: 那也没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: We work with those creators.
zh: 我们与那些创作者合作。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's other creators, a growing number of them I would say, who are very interested in exploring, wait, can these GenAI tools make something possible that wasn't possible before?
zh: 还有一些创作者，我想说越来越多，非常有兴趣探索，等等，这些 GenAI 工具能让以前不可能的事情变得可能吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I tell a story in a new way?
zh: 我能用一种新的方式讲故事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I make that story higher quality and more resonant for audiences?
zh: 我能让那个故事质量更高、更能引起观众的共鸣吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I do things that are extra creative in how I think about bringing a story to life?
zh: 在我考虑如何将故事呈现出来的方式上，我能做一些额外有创意的事情吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And we support them as well and we support all the folks who are in the in between.
zh: 我们也支持他们，我们也支持所有处于中间地带的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's a really important position for us to be in, again, because entertainment is not going to be one thing.
zh: 我认为这对我们来说是一个非常重要的立场，同样因为娱乐不会只有一种形式。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's not going to be one format.
zh: 不会只有一种格式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's going to be types of film and TV that feel traditional, and then there's going to be entirely new formats that unbelievable creators help to bring to life.
zh: 我认为会有感觉传统的影视类型，然后会有惊人的创作者帮助实现的全新的格式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Netflix wants to participate in that, which means we need to have a flexibility in the tools that we provide, and the types of partnerships we have, and to really have a creator enablement view, rather than a prescriptive, we only do this one way.
zh: Netflix 想要参与其中，这意味着我们需要在提供的工具和建立的合作伙伴关系类型上具有灵活性，并且真正要有创作者赋能的观点，而不是规定性的，我们只用这一种方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think people are going to be surprised by just how good AI content is.
zh: 我认为人们会对 AI 内容有多好感到惊讶。

speaker: Unknown
en: Spencer Pratt's videos are just like... everyone's like, wow, this is entertaining.
zh: Spencer Pratt 的视频就像……每个人都说，哇，这很有娱乐性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously, AI, but it's so interesting.
zh: 显然是 AI，但这太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think we'll get to a place where just whole TV shows are AI and people love it?
zh: 你认为我们会达到这样一个境地吗，整个电视节目都是 AI 做的，而人们很喜欢？

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a hard time picturing entertainment that doesn't have humans at the heart of it.
zh: 我很难想象没有人类作为核心的娱乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's humans in the creation of the storytelling, which I think is a scarce and valuable skill.
zh: 所以，那是人类在故事创作中，我认为这是一种稀缺且有价值的技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Storytelling is one and the same with humanity and knowing what connects with people.
zh: 讲故事与人类是一回事，并且知道什么能与人产生联系。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I think humans will always be a core part or a critical part of the story.
zh: 所以，我认为人类将永远是故事的核心部分或关键部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think watching characters on screen who don't have that humanity feels less compelling to me.
zh: 我认为看屏幕上那些没有那种人性的角色对我来说感觉不那么有吸引力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what the power of storytelling really is, to see another human and to watch how they perform a role or bring an emotion to life, that's such a human element.
zh: 讲故事的真正力量在于，看到另一个人，观看他们如何演绎一个角色或将情感呈现出来，那是如此人性化的元素。

speaker: Unknown
en: Will AI help to bring that to life?
zh: AI 会帮助将其呈现出来吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Will it play a material part in some of those productions or how we get them to look and feel a certain way?
zh: AI 会在其中发挥实质性作用吗？无论是制作本身还是我们要让它看起来或感觉起来是某种特定方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, definitely.
zh: 是的，绝对是。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I don't see the version of it that doesn't have the human as the backbone.
zh: 但我不认为那是一种没有人类作为骨干的版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a quote that I think is misattributed to Salman Rushdie, which is, "When a child is born, they first ask for food and water and protection and then they ask for, tell me a story."
zh: 有一句话我认为是误认为是 Salman Rushdie 说的，那就是，“当一个孩子出生时，他们首先要求食物、水和保护，然后他们要求，给我讲个故事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a thing going back since the beginning of time, that storytelling has been a key part of community, and social networks, and human feeling and connection.
zh: 自时间开始以来，讲故事一直是社区、社交网络、人类情感和连接的关键部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I love the idea that technology can amplify that and can bring that to life in very new, novel, exciting ways.
zh: 所以，我喜欢技术可以放大这一点，并可以以非常新的、新颖的、令人兴奋的方式将其呈现出来的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: But to say storytelling wouldn't have that humanity at the center feels like something would be missing.
zh: 但如果说讲故事的核心没有那种人性，感觉就像是缺失了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to see some wild shit over the years coming out of all this.
zh: 在未来几年里，我们将看到从这一切中产生的一些疯狂的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, there's no question about that.
zh: 哦，毫无疑问。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a lot of it could be very entertaining.
zh: 其中很多可能会非常有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't debate that either.
zh: 我也不否认这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think there's going to be a broad range and I think Netflix needs to be at the center of shaping that and bringing that to life, which is our plan.
zh: 但我认为会有很广泛的范围，我认为 Netflix 需要处于塑造这一局面和将其呈现出来的中心，这就是我们的计划。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, we covered a lot of ground, Elizabeth.
zh: 好的，我们涵盖了很多内容，Elizabeth。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before we get to our very exciting lightning round, is there anything else that you wanted to share, leave listeners with, maybe double down on some things we've talked about?
zh: 在我们进入非常激动人心的闪电战之前，你还有什么其他想分享的，想留给听众的，也许想强调我们谈到的某些内容吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It probably came across throughout, but I would underscore that this is a really exciting time to be building products in entertainment.
zh: 这可能在整个过程中都传达出来了，但我想强调，这是在娱乐领域构建产品的一个非常令人兴奋的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everything we talked about of what's changing in the tech and consumers and what is entertainment.
zh: 我们谈论的关于技术、消费者以及什么是娱乐的所有变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're at this unbelievable high velocity innovation period, so it's what keeps me at Netflix.
zh: 我们正处于这个难以置信的高速度创新时期，这就是我留在 Netflix 的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's a fun place to be.
zh: 我认为这是一个有趣的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would be missing something if I didn't reinforce that I think that's true.
zh: 如果我不强调我认为这是真的，那我就漏掉了一些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also think that as an industry, we spend a lot of time sometimes talking about the pure tech or the capability and we sort of lose the forest for the trees.
zh: 我也认为作为一个行业，我们有时花很多时间谈论纯技术或能力，我们有点见树不见林。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're trying to build great consumer products that people love.
zh: 我们正努力打造人们喜爱的优秀消费级产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're trying to make great entertainment that people love and it's their favorite thing, that I don't want that to be lost in.
zh: 我们正努力制作人们喜爱的优秀娱乐内容,那是他们最喜爱的事物,我不希望那部分迷失在其中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, there's amazing tech and product stuff that sits underneath, but in the end, the thing that's most inspirational is what do we bring to people around the world.
zh: 当然,底层依托着令人惊叹的技术和产品,但归根结底,最鼓舞人心的是我们为全世界的人们带来了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And along those lines, there's been such a... the opposite of glut and drought of a consumer, new consumer products, consumer experiences.
zh: 顺着这个思路,出现了如此多的……与消费级新产品、消费级体验的泛滥和匮乏相反的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's very few success.
zh: 成功的案例寥寥无几。

speaker: Unknown
en: Almost no consumer startup works and AI feels like an opportunity for something else to work.
zh: 几乎没有消费级初创公司能成功,而 AI 感觉像是另一种可能行得通的机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like Netflix is one of the rare companies and brands that continues to deliver an awesome consumer product and business.
zh: 我觉得 Netflix 是少数几家能持续交付优秀消费级产品和业务的稀有公司和品牌之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just not that many of them.

speaker: Unknown
en: So, good job.
zh: 所以,干得漂亮。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to keep that up.

speaker: Unknown

en: I don't remember what they were, but that sounds great.

speaker: Unknown
en: I still like a good throwback.

speaker: Unknown

en: So, the risk of repeating, maybe.

en: First, something good happens every day.

speaker: Unknown
en: Watch for it, even in the most stressful times.

speaker: Unknown
en: And second, that the last 5% of effort usually makes all the difference.

speaker: Unknown
en: These are awesome.

speaker: Unknown
en: They hit me.

en: I love cycling.

speaker: Unknown
en: It's fun to be able to participate in it.

en: You go nonchalantly.

en: So, I got to be comfortable enough to hold my own.

en: Wow.

en: So, that's a good first stop, usually.
